source_text.text:
[PDF_PAGE 11 | score=13] Wood: 10% cost; 50% weight; -2 damage; -3 to attack roll. The damage modifier reduces the damage normally done by the weapon, with a mini- mum of 1 point of damage. The attack roll modifier does not apply to missile weapons, as the attack roll reflects the character’s aim and is not a function of the material used to make the weapon. Damage modifiers do apply to missile weapons, however. Enchanted nonmetallic weapons must overcome the negative modifiers, too; thus a bone dagger +1 works just as well as a normal steel dagger. Whenever a nonmetallic weapon inflicts maximum damage in combat, it has a 1 in 20 chance of breaking and becoming useless. (The DM rolls a d20.) Nonweapon and Weapon Pro
[PDF_PAGE 98 | score=6] determines the size of the new treant, which possesses the caster’s memories and person- ality but has no granted powers or spellcast- ing ability. It must communicate as a treant. The DM decides whether to consider this treant an NPC or allow the player to control it. (DMs should use the guidelines that apply to PCs who become lycanthropes or undead.) However, when a druid uses tree spirit to link with a tree, the character suffers any physical damage inflicted on the tree. For instance, if someone hacks at the tree with an axe and causes 4 points of damage, the caster also loses 4 hit points; the druid knows the tree has been harmed, but does not know the nature of the injury. If the tree 
[PDF_PAGE 104 | score=6] victim. Either 10,20, or 30 insects may swarm over a victim per attack; for every 10 insects that hit, the victim loses ldlO hit points, and the wearer of the crown loses 1 hp-each insect dies after its attack. So, a character may inflict up to 3d10 points of damage per attack at the cost of 3 hp. While in insect form, the user has a move of 3, but can climb walls and ceilings. The user cannot employ any weapons, spells, magical items, tools, or armor. The swarm, which has AC 0, suffers no damage from piercing weap om (E'), 1 point of damage from slashing weap ons (S), and half damage from bludgeoning weapons (8). Magical bonuses and fire inflict full damage. Every point of damage to the wea
[PDF_PAGE 94 | score=5] The spell's material component is a pinch of the food that can end the spell. I Knurl (Alteration) Sphere: Plant Range: 5 yards/level of caster Components: V, S, M Duration: 1 turn/level of caster Casting Tie: 7 Area of Effect: 1 person Saving Throw: Neg. Casting a knurl spell transforms a mature's arm into a tree branch of the same thickness, covered with bark and twigs. The new limb possesses neither elbow nor wrist join6not even a hand. The "arm" remains attached to the shoulder. The spell's recipient can use it as a club but not to manipulate tools, weap- ons, or spell components. The caster chooses which of the recipient's arms to affect. A character could use multiple knurl spells to t
[PDF_PAGE 10 | score=4] aurials, and swanrnays. s can become druids, a regions of a campaign. They could reach the 7th level of ability. Again, players could develop a druidlike priest kit for this race. Halflings. The AD&D Original Edition game allowed halflings to reach the 6th level. of ability as NPCs; Umarthcd Arcana allowed* halfling druid PCs to reach higher levels. In", the AD&D 2nd Edition Complefe Book of". tree, but it remains unlikely that elf druids can reach 12th level (like r ch with having fun to side of a druid's life. the sickle used in the harvest and the crescent moon, which stands for birth, death, and rebirth in the cycle of Nature. The standard druid can use the following weapons: club, sickl
[PDF_PAGE 95 | score=4] cannot use this particular spell to propel the cloud at all, a control minds spell can summon a great gust of air to turn the cloudscape into a unique flying conveyance easily enough. Nature’s Charm (F,nchantment/Charm) Sphere: Elemental (earth, water) Range: Touch Components: V, S, M Duration: 2 hours/level of caster Casting Tie: 1 round Area of Effect: 15-foot radius/level of caster Saving Throw: Creatures native to the area of effect are not affected. Nature’s charm causes a particular place to exert a special fascination beyond mere beauty to anyone entering the area except the spellcaster. This spell must target a site of notable nat- ural splendor that possesses both edible plants and 
[PDF_PAGE 100 | score=3] Wands and staves Wand of Shape Binding. Characters often use this item againsf druids. When hit by its multicolored beam (projected up to 80 feet), beings with the ability to shapechange or polymorph must save vs. wands at a -3 penalty. Victims who fail cannot voluntarily aIter shape for 2d10 turns. Attempts to shift shape using spells, magical items, or innate powers result in failure. A use of this rechargeable wand consumes one charge per 4 HD or levels of the subject. XP value: 800. Wanderer’s Staff. This resembles a stout oaken staff, which radiates magic and, in fact, functions as a quarterstaff+l. However, its pri- mary power is locomotion. If carried as a walking stick, users hiking 
[PDF_PAGE 103 | score=3] formation to a serpent of the tree is nearly complete; only a wish can return the victim to normal then. The serpent tree remains permanently he= planted and stays loyal to its maker as as it exists. Serpent of the tree: Int Animal (1); AL N; AC5; MV 15; HD 2+1; THACO 19; #AT 1; Dmg 1; ML 8; sz s (5 ft.); Xp90. Stone of Lost Ways. This pebble might be mistaken for any other magical stone.
